resources.string: Align the UPC and Description columns\ninside the crop guide
Make sure all rows are visible and readable
Camera permission is required to scan manifests and barcodes.
Camera permission was denied. Please enable it in Settings to use this app.

This app works completely offline. It requires no Wi-Fi, no cellular data, and no internet connection. Everything runs entirely on the phone.

All photos taken during use are temporary. They exist in the phone's memory only long enough to read the text or barcode, then they are immediately discarded.

No images are saved to the phone's storage.
No data is sent to any server.
No personal information is collected.
No account or login is required.

The app uses Google's machine learning libraries (ML Kit) for reading text and scanning barcodes. These libraries are licensed under the Google APIs Terms of Service and are free to use with no fees and no attribution required. All processing happens on the phone itself.

1. Print the manifest single-sided. Number the rows every 5 items (1, 5, 10, 15, 20...).

2. Tap Capture Manifest. Position the crop guide over the UPC and Description columns. Tap the camera button.

3. Verify the cropped image looks right. Tap Process OCR.

4. Review the page results. Tap Accept if it looks correct, or Retry to recapture.

5. Tap Next Page for additional pages. Overlap slightly with the previous page.

6. Type the expected item count from the manifest footer. Check for warnings.

7. Tap Start Scanning. Point at a barcode and tap the scan button.

8. The app shows the item name and position number. Verify against your paper manifest.

9. After scanning all boxes, tap View Report to check the Multiples tab for items with more than one case.

WHAT THIS APP DOES

Manifest Sync helps you verify physical deliveries against printed manifests. Scan the manifest with OCR, then match barcodes on boxes to quickly locate each item on your list. You photograph the printed delivery manifest, and the app reads the UPC numbers using your phone's camera. Then you scan barcodes on the physical boxes, and the app tells you which item you're holding and where it appears on the manifest.


BEFORE YOU START

Print the manifest single-sided. Double-sided printing makes it harder to look up items while scanning.

Number the manifest. Write position numbers in the margin every 5 rows (1, 5, 10, 15, 20...). This helps you quickly find items when the app gives you a position number.

Have a pen ready. You will mark items on the paper manifest and mark boxes on the cart as you work.

Tap Help on the home screen before starting to review this guide, including privacy information for managers.


CAPTURING THE MANIFEST

Tap Capture Manifest. The camera opens with a white rectangle (the crop guide) overlaid on the screen.

Align the crop guide so it covers the UPC number column and the item description column. You do not need to include the Cases column.

Drag corners or edges to resize the rectangle. Tap inside the rectangle and drag to move it without resizing.

The Light button (top right) toggles the flashlight. The Cancel button (bottom left) returns to the previous screen without losing data.

After capturing, verify the cropped image shows all rows clearly. Tap Process OCR to read the text, or Retry to recapture.

Review the page results. An orange warning banner appears if the app detects a possible misread. Tap Accept to add items to your list, or Retry to discard and recapture.

For additional pages, tap Next Page. A green banner shows the last item from the previous page so you know where to start. Overlap slightly with the previous page to avoid missing items at the boundary. The app automatically removes duplicate UPCs.

After all pages, type the expected item count from the manifest footer. If counts don't match, an orange warning appears.

Tap any item to edit its quantity (all default to 1 case).


SCANNING BARCODES

Tap Start Scanning. The camera opens zoomed in for barcode reading. The Light button (top right) toggles the flashlight.

Point at a barcode and tap the scan button (magnifying glass). The app searches for about 2 seconds. Hold the phone steady.

The app reads both the standard barcode on the side of boxes and the long shipping barcode on case labels.

If a match is found, the item name and position number are shown. Tap Confirm or Reject.


BARCODE LOOKUP MODE (RECOMMENDED)

The paper manifest is your official record. The app is a lookup tool.

1. Pick up a box, mark a slash (/) on the corner
2. Scan the barcode
3. Note the position number
4. Find it on your paper manifest and circle it
5. Complete the slash into an X on the box

After scanning everything, tap View Report and check the Multiples tab. Items listed there were scanned more than once (multiple cases arrived). Note the quantities on your paper manifest.

Write the date, your name, and delivery status on the paper manifest.


DIGITAL TRACKING MODE

The app tracks scan counts automatically. After scanning all boxes, tap View Report.

The Missing tab shows items not yet scanned.
The Multiples tab shows items scanned more than once.
The Extra tab shows barcodes not on the manifest.


TROUBLESHOOTING

"No barcode detected" happens often: Hold the phone closer and keep it very still for the full 2 seconds.

OCR missed some items: Check the expected items count warning. Recapture the section that was missed, overlapping with neighboring pages.

Item descriptions look wrong: This is normal. The phone reads text column by column and sometimes combines words from different items. The UPC numbers are correct.

App matched the wrong item: Some products have nearly identical UPC numbers. Always verify the position number against your paper manifest. Tap Reject if it doesn't match.

App crashes: Reopen and start over. In Barcode Lookup mode, your paper manifest still has all your progress.


TECHNICAL NOTES

The app uses Google's ML Kit libraries for text recognition and barcode scanning, licensed under the Google APIs Terms of Service (free, no fees, no attribution required). All processing happens on the phone. No data is sent anywhere. No images are saved.

Designed and directed by Paul Henkiel, with code generated using AI assistance.

Version 0.7.0
